question.text: A farmer expects to harvest wheat in 3 months and wants to lock in a minimum selling price. Which option strategy is appropriate?

answer.text: Buy put options on wheat to guarantee a minimum selling price
Buy call options on wheat to benefit from price increases
Sell call options on wheat to earn premium income
Sell put options on wheat to lock in the purchase price

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The farmer wants to SELL wheat → needs protection against FALLING prices. Buy put options: guarantees a minimum selling price (strike price). - If price falls below strike: exercise put, sell at strike price - If price rises: don't exercise put, sell at higher market price (lose premium only)
**The Trap:** Buying calls is for buyers (consumers), not sellers (producers). Wrong direction.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Producers hedge with puts (sell protection). Consumers hedge with calls (buy protection).
**Key Takeaway:** Seller/producer hedging = buy puts. Guarantees minimum selling price = strike - premium.
